summary: Improper use of a grid may lead to excess scattering causing film fogging issues due to misalignment between the grid and imaging conditions. Even well-configured grids contribute minimally towards secondary radiation fog compared to their intended purpose of reducing scatter. The main issue lies within potential user error affecting overall performance efficiency negatively through unintentional increases in unwanted exposure components like stray photons reaching sensitive areas post-primary beam interaction.
